response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a broad background in web, mobile, and embedded domains, but has no discernible experience in any of the core competencies required for this role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, deep learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The role demands principal-level expertise at the intersection of hardware architecture and AI systems, a domain this candidate has not worked in at any level.
strengths: 14+ years of continuous software engineering experience across diverse platforms, demonstrating adaptability and longevity
Some exposure to low-latency and high-throughput systems at Sociomantic (async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, high-performance databases)
Demonstrated interest in systems-level programming through use of DLang, C/C++, and embedded/hardware-adjacent work at National Instruments and Altom
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or any form of hardware performance modeling — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI, machine learning, deep neural networks, or GPU workloads; AI-agent work at re:cinq is at the application/tooling layer, not the hardware or inference-optimization layer

Education is a BS in Computer Science (2012); while this meets the threshold with 8+ years of experience, that experience is entirely in web and application software, not in the relevant hardware/AI domain

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/DL workload or accelerator expertise
